perturbation.original_text: 1. EVALUATION MATERIAL. The term "Evaluation Material" also shall be deemed to include all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by each party or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished to such party or its Representatives pursuant hereto which is not available to the general public. The term "Evaluation Material" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or its Representatives; (ii) was within the receiving party's possession prior to its being furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party, provided that the source of such information was not known by the receiving party to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the disclosing party or any other party with respect to such information; (iii) is or becomes available to the receiving party on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the disclosing party, or any of its Representatives, provided that such source was not known by
perturbation.changed_text: 1. EVALUATION MATERIAL. The term "Evaluation Material" also shall be deemed to include all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by each party or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished to such party or its Representatives pursuant hereto which is not available to the general public. The term "Evaluation Material" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public regardless of whether a breach of this Agreement caused it; (ii) was within the receiving party's possession prior to its being furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party, provided that the source of such information was not known by the receiving party to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the disclosing party or any other party with respect to such information; (iii) is or becomes available to the receiving party on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the disclosing party, or any of its Representatives, provided that such source was not known by
perturbation.explanation: The change to the definition of 'Evaluation Material' in Section 1 now states that information available to the public is excluded from the definition of 'Evaluation Material', regardless of whether that information became public due to a breach of the agreement by the receiving party or its Representatives. This contradicts the original condition which excluded information from the definition only if it became public other than as a result of a breach of the agreement, leading to an internal contradiction if the breach of the agreement led to the material becoming available to the public.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. EVALUATION MATERIAL. The term "Evaluation Material" also shall be deemed to include all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by each party or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished to such party or its Representatives pursuant hereto which is not available to the general public. The term "Evaluation Material" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public regardless of whether a breach of this Agreement caused it; (ii) was within the receiving party's possession prior to its being furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party, provided that the source of such information was not known by the receiving party to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the disclosing party or any other party with respect to such information; (iii) is or becomes available to the receiving party on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the disclosing party, or any of its Representatives, provided that such source was not known by
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Evaluation Material' to exclude information that becomes publicly available 'regardless of whether a breach of this Agreement caused it'. The original text excluded information only if it became public *other than* as a result of a breach. This directly contradicts the idea that the receiving party can disclose confidential information and then argue that because of the disclosure, it is no longer confidential, even if that disclosure was a breach of the agreement. Since the Contradicted Text is in the same location as the Changed Text and the Changed Text creates a contradiction, the determination is a YES.

perturbation.original_text: 2. USE OF EVALUATION MATERIAL. Each party hereby agrees that it and its Representatives shall use the other party's Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating and consummating a possible transaction between the parties, and that the disclosing party's Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and each party and its Representatives will not disclose or use for purposes other than the evaluation, negotiation and consummation of a transaction any of the other party's Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) the receiving party may make any disclosure of such information to which the disclosing party gives its prior written consent and (ii) any of such information may be disclosed to the receiving party's Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of assisting the receiving party in evaluating, negotiating and consummating a possible transaction between the parties (it being understood that such Representatives shall be informed by the receiving party of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by the receiving party to treat such information confidentially). In any event, the receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives and the receiving party agrees, at its sole expense, to take all reasonable measures (including but not limited to court proceedings) to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. USE OF EVALUATION MATERIAL. Each party hereby agrees that it and its Representatives may use the other party's Evaluation Material for any purpose, including but not limited to evaluating, negotiating and consummating a possible transaction between the parties, and that the disclosing party's Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and each party and its Representatives will not disclose or use for purposes other than the evaluation, negotiation and consummation of a transaction any of the other party's Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) the receiving party may make any disclosure of such information to which the disclosing party gives its prior written consent and (ii) any of such information may be disclosed to the receiving party's Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of assisting the receiving party in evaluating, negotiating and consummating a possible transaction between the parties (it being understood that such Representatives shall be informed by the receiving party of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by the receiving party to treat such information confidentially). In any event, the receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives and the receiving party agrees, at its sole expense, to take all reasonable measures (including but not limited to court proceedings) to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 2 now states that Evaluation Material may be used for 'any purpose' which include evaluating, negotiating and consummating a possible transaction between the parties, this creates a contradiction with the rest of the paragraph that says: '...and each party and its Representatives will not disclose or use for purposes other than the evaluation, negotiation and consummation of a transaction...' therefore, it is unclear for what purpose the evaluation material can be used
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. USE OF EVALUATION MATERIAL. Each party hereby agrees that it and its Representatives may use the other party's Evaluation Material for any purpose, including but not limited to evaluating, negotiating and consummating a possible transaction between the parties, and that the disclosing party's Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and each party and its Representatives will not disclose or use for purposes other than the evaluation, negotiation and consummation of a transaction any of the other party's Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) the receiving party may make any disclosure of such information to which the disclosing party gives its prior written consent and (ii) any of such information may be disclosed to the receiving party's Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of assisting the receiving party in evaluating, negotiating and consummating a possible transaction between the parties (it being understood that such Representatives shall be informed by the receiving party of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by the receiving party to treat such information confidentially). In any event, the receiving party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives and the receiving party agrees, at its sole expense, to take all reasonable measures (including but not limited to court proceedings) to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2 introduces a contradiction. It first states that Evaluation Material may be used for 'any purpose', but then it states that it 'will not disclose or use for purposes other than the evaluation, negotiation and consummation of a transaction'. The contradicted text is the same section as the changed text. This creates a strong internal contradiction within the same clause. It is unclear for what purpose the evaluation material can be used since any purpose contradicts only for a limited purpose. Therefore, based on the provided information, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 3. NON-DISCLOSURE. In addition, each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, it and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person the fact that the Evaluation Material has been made available hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto, including the status thereof; provided, however, that a party may make such disclosure if, in the written opinion of such party's outside legal counsel, such disclosure is necessary in order to comply with, or avoid committing a violation of, any law or any rule of any applicable stock exchange or system.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. NON-DISCLOSURE. In addition, each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, it and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person the fact that the Evaluation Material has been made available hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto, including the status thereof; provided, however, that a party may make such disclosure if they feel it is in their best interest.
perturbation.explanation: The change made in Section 3 removed the condition that disclosure must be based on legal counsel's opinion and instead allows disclosure if a party 'feels it is in their best interest'. This introduces an internal contradiction because the section initially restricts disclosure unless legally required, but then permits it based on subjective self-interest, this uncertainty undermines the original intention of strict non-disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. NON-DISCLOSURE. In addition, each party agrees that, without the prior written consent of the other party, it and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person the fact that the Evaluation Material has been made available hereunder, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction involving the parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto, including the status thereof; provided, however, that a party may make such disclosure if they feel it is in their best interest.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 3 restricts disclosure unless legally required. The changed text of Section 3 allows disclosure if a party 'feels it is in their best interest', which introduces a contradiction because it permits disclosure based on subjective self-interest, thus undermining the original intention of strict non-disclosure, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted text are at the same location, but the change introduces a contradiction, the answer is 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: 5. TERMINATION OF DISCUSSIONS. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a transaction with the other party, the party so deciding will promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving written notice thereof. In that case, or at any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party will promptly redeliver to the disclosing party all written Evaluation Material furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto, and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. All documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon the information in the Evaluation Material shall be destroyed or, at the receiving party's election, shall be delivered to outside legal counsel of the receiving party (where such materials shall be held in confidence, except as needed in the context of actual or threatened legal proceedings, or destroyed), and such destruction or delivery to outside counsel shall be certified in writing to the disclosing party by an authorized officer of the receiving party supervising such destruction or making such delivery to outside counsel.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. TERMINATION OF DISCUSSIONS. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a transaction with the other party, the party so deciding will promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving written notice thereof. In that case, or at any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party may choose whether or not to redeliver to the disclosing party all written Evaluation Material furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto, and may retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. All documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon the information in the Evaluation Material may or may not be destroyed or, at the receiving party's election, may or may not be delivered to outside legal counsel of the receiving party (where such materials shall be held in confidence, except as needed in the context of actual or threatened legal proceedings, or destroyed), and such destruction or delivery to outside counsel may or may not be certified in writing to the disclosing party by an authorized officer of the receiving party supervising such destruction or making such delivery to outside counsel.
perturbation.explanation: The change to Section 5 introduces uncertainty by making the return of Evaluation Material, the destruction of documents, and the certification of destruction optional ('may choose whether or not to'). This contradicts the original mandatory language ('will promptly redeliver,' 'shall be destroyed,' 'shall be certified'), creating an internal contradiction regarding the receiving party's obligations upon termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. TERMINATION OF DISCUSSIONS. If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a transaction with the other party, the party so deciding will promptly inform the other party of that decision by giving written notice thereof. In that case, or at any time upon the request of either disclosing party for any reason, each receiving party may choose whether or not to redeliver to the disclosing party all written Evaluation Material furnished to the receiving party or its Representatives by or on behalf of the disclosing party pursuant hereto, and may retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. All documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the receiving party or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon the information in the Evaluation Material may or may not be destroyed or, at the receiving party's election, may or may not be delivered to outside legal counsel of the receiving party (where such materials shall be held in confidence, except as needed in the context of actual or threatened legal proceedings, or destroyed), and such destruction or delivery to outside counsel may or may not be certified in writing to the disclosing party by an authorized officer of the receiving party supervising such destruction or making such delivery to outside counsel.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5 uses mandatory language ('will promptly redeliver', 'shall be destroyed', 'shall be certified'). The changed text replaces this with optional language ('may choose whether or not to', 'may or may not be'). The contradicted text is the same changed text, making the obligations of the receiving party uncertain and creating a direct in-text contradiction within the same section regarding what is required upon termination of discussions. This is also aligned with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 6. REPRESENTATION OF ACCURACY. Each party understands and acknowledges that neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material made available by it or to it. Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of such other party's Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. REPRESENTATION OF ACCURACY. Each party understands and acknowledges that neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material made available by it or to it, except for information intentionally misrepresented. Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of such other party's Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct. Only those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 6 adds exceptions to the disclaimer of liability and warranty by including clauses such as 'except for information intentionally misrepresented' and 'except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct'. This directly contradicts the initial statement that 'neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty' and 'neither party...shall have any liability,' thereby creating an internal contradiction. The addition introduces scenarios where liability and warranty could exist, thus conflicting with the original blanket disclaimer. It also causes an uncertainty on cases that could happen regarding of those exception.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. REPRESENTATION OF ACCURACY. Each party understands and acknowledges that neither party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material made available by it or to it, except for information intentionally misrepresented. Each party agrees that neither party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the other party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of such other party's Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct. Only those representations or warranties which are made in a final definitive agreement regarding the transaction, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6 provided a blanket disclaimer of liability and warranty regarding the accuracy of Evaluation Material. The changed text modifies this section by introducing exceptions to this disclaimer, specifically 'except for information intentionally misrepresented' and 'except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct'. This directly contradicts the original blanket disclaimer because it now acknowledges scenarios where liability and warranty *can* exist, thus creating an internal inconsistency within Section 6 itself. Since Section 6 is also the contradicted text, and the explanation details how a contradiction was introduced in the changed version, it causes a clear contradiction in the document, making enforcement unclear and introducing uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: 7. NON-SOLICITATION. In consideration of the furnishing of Evaluation Material hereunder, each party agrees that, for a period of two years from the date hereof, neither such party nor any of its controlled affiliates will solicit to employ any of the current officers or employees of the other party with whom such party has contact or who is specifically identified to such party during the period of such party's investigation of the other party with respect to a possible transaction, so long as they are employed by the other party, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party; provided, however, that solicitation for purposes of this paragraph shall not include solicitation of employees or officers (i) who first solicit employment from such party, or (ii) who are solicited (A) by advertising in periodicals of general circulation, <PAGE> Ricoh Company, Ltd. June 22, 2000 Page 4 or (B) by an employee search firm on behalf of such party, so long as such party did not directly encourage such firm to solicit such employee or officer or any other employees or officers of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. NON-SOLICITATION. In consideration of the furnishing of Evaluation Material hereunder, each party agrees that, for a period of two years from the date hereof, neither such party nor any of its controlled affiliates will solicit to employ any of the current officers or employees of the other party with whom such party has contact or who is specifically identified to such party during the period of such party's investigation of the other party with respect to a possible transaction, so long as they are employed by the other party, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party; provided, however, that solicitation for purposes of this paragraph shall include solicitation of employees or officers (i) who first solicit employment from such party, or (ii) who are solicited (A) by advertising in periodicals of general circulation, <PAGE> Ricoh Company, Ltd. June 22, 2000 Page 4 or (B) by an employee search firm on behalf of such party, so long as such party did not directly encourage such firm to solicit such employee or officer or any other employees or officers of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 7, the modification changed 'shall not include' to 'shall include,' reversing the meaning of the exceptions to the non-solicitation agreement. Previously, these were circumstances under which solicitation was permitted without consent. Now, these scenarios are considered solicitation, requiring prior written consent. This change causes the exceptions to contradict the opening statement, which states parties cannot solicit to employ, unless they have prior witten consent. Now the conditions are now considered solicitations. This creates an internal contradiction, because, the situations stated in the exception, are now considered solicitation and the opening statement dictates those situations needed of prior written consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. NON-SOLICITATION. In consideration of the furnishing of Evaluation Material hereunder, each party agrees that, for a period of two years from the date hereof, neither such party nor any of its controlled affiliates will solicit to employ any of the current officers or employees of the other party with whom such party has contact or who is specifically identified to such party during the period of such party's investigation of the other party with respect to a possible transaction, so long as they are employed by the other party, without obtaining the prior written consent of the other party; provided, however, that solicitation for purposes of this paragraph shall include solicitation of employees or officers (i) who first solicit employment from such party, or (ii) who are solicited (A) by advertising in periodicals of general circulation, <PAGE> Ricoh Company, Ltd. June 22, 2000 Page 4 or (B) by an employee search firm on behalf of such party, so long as such party did not directly encourage such firm to solicit such employee or officer or any other employees or officers of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The modification in Section 7 changes 'shall not include' to 'shall include,' effectively reversing the permitted exceptions to the non-solicitation agreement. The original text allowed for solicitation under specific conditions (employee-initiated contact, general advertising, or use of a search firm). The changed text now classifies these scenarios as solicitation, thereby requiring prior written consent. This directly contradicts the beginning of Section 7, which states that parties cannot solicit employees without prior written consent; the modified exceptions now fall under this prohibition, creating a clear in-text contradiction and thus impacting the enforceability and understanding of the agreement.
